format: Author-date in-text (Author, Year) with full reference list at end; 7th edition of the APA Publication Manual (2020).
when_to_use: When writing in psychology, education, social sciences, business, nursing, and most behavioral sciences.
example: In-text: (Smith, 2020, p. 45) / Reference list: Smith, J. (2020). Title of work. Publisher.
source_name: American Psychological Association — APA Style 7th Edition (2020)

format: Numbered in-text citations in square brackets [1], [2], [3] with full reference list at end ordered by citation appearance; IEEE Editorial Style Manual.
when_to_use: When writing in electrical engineering, computer engineering, telecommunications, and other IEEE-published fields.
example: In-text: [1] / Reference: [1] J. Smith, "Title," in Proc. IEEE Conf., 2020, pp. 45-50.

format: Numbered in-text citations [1] with reference list; ACM Reference Format, used for all ACM-published articles.
when_to_use: When writing in computer science and submitting to ACM conferences or journals (SIGCHI, KDD, ICSE, etc.).
example: In-text: [1] / Reference: [1] Jane Smith. 2020. Title. In Proceedings of ACM Conf. (CONF '20). ACM, NY, 45-50.
source_name: Association for Computing Machinery — Reference Formatting

format: Parenthetical in-text (Author Page) with Works Cited list; MLA Handbook (9th edition, 2021).
when_to_use: When writing in literature, languages, humanities, and arts.
example: In-text: (Smith 45) / Works Cited: Smith, John. Title of Work. Publisher, 2020.
source_name: Modern Language Association — MLA Style Center (9th edition, 2021)

format: Two variants: (1) Notes and Bibliography (footnotes/endnotes with full bibliography); (2) Author-Date (Author Year). The Chicago Manual of Style (17th edition, 2017).
when_to_use: When writing in history, theology, arts, and humanities (Notes-Bibliography); or in physical sciences (Author-Date).
example: Note: 1. John Smith, Title (City: Publisher, 2020), 45. / Bibliography: Smith, John. Title. City: Publisher, 2020.
source_name: The Chicago Manual of Style (17th edition, 2017) — University of Chicago Press

format: Reference entries in .bib files using @type{key, fields...}; cited in LaTeX documents with \cite{key}. Used with bibtex or biber engines.
when_to_use: When writing academic papers in LaTeX, especially in computer science, mathematics, physics, and engineering; for reproducible bibliographies.
example: @article{smith2020, author = {John Smith}, title = {Title}, journal = {Journal}, year = {2020}, volume = {5}, pages = {45-50}}

format: Structured metadata in JSON-LD format using Schema.org vocabulary (e.g., ScholarlyArticle, Citation, CreativeWork) embedded in HTML.
when_to_use: When publishing scholarly content online so that search engines and indexing services (Google Scholar, OpenAlex) can parse citation metadata and link related works.

format: Numbered footnote references using <ref> tags and {{cite}} templates rendered as [1], [2] in the article body with auto-generated reference list.
when_to_use: When contributing to Wikipedia or mirroring its citation style in lightweight wikis; not for academic publishing but ubiquitous for web reference works.
example: Article body: The sky is blue.<ref name="smith2020">{{Cite web | url=https://example.com | title=Source | access-date=2024-01-01}}</ref>
